title: Shortcut Keys (Ctrl / Command)
drDigitalIntro: Every computer has a special key for keyboard shortcuts. On most computers (Windows, Chromebook, Linux), it's Ctrl. On Mac, it's Command. Hold this key and press another letter to do something quickly — like Ctrl+S to save, Ctrl+C to copy, or Ctrl+V to paste. These shortcuts work in almost every app!
drDigitalSuccess: That's your shortcut key — Ctrl (or Command on Mac). It powers almost every handy shortcut!
drDigitalHint: Look for Ctrl in the bottom-left corner of your keyboard (or Command next to the space bar on Mac), then click Continue.
